{"problem_standardized_math":"Assume that the basis matrix is given by the first $m$ columns of $A$, so that $P=I$ in (15.7), and define\n\\[\nY=\\begin{bmatrix}\nI\\\\\n(B^{-1}N)^T\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nZ=\\begin{bmatrix}\n-B^{-1}N\\\\\nI\n\\end{bmatrix}.\n\\tag{15.37}\n\\]\nShow that the columns of $Y$ and $Z$ do not, in general, have norm $1$, and that the relations $AZ=0$ and $Y^T Z=0$ hold. Hence show that the columns of $Y$ and $Z$ are linearly independent, so that (15.37) gives a valid choice of basis matrices."}
